heroText: Dodoma is a capital city in Tanzania, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Dodoma
highlights.text: Dodoma is a capital city in Tanzania, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: The city, designed over 2,500 acres (1,000 hectares), was meant to be "the chief village in a nation of villages", built at a human scale meant to be experienced on foot.

history.label: 20th century
history.text: 20th century Originally a small market town known as Idodomya, the modern Dodoma was founded in 1907 by German colonists during construction of the Tanzanian central railway.
The site, the Dodoma region, had been looked at as a potential new capital as early as 1915 by the then colonial power Germany, in 1932 by the British as a League of Nations.

In 1967, following independence, the government invited Canadian firm Project Planning Associates Ltd to draw up a master plan to help control and organise the then capital of the country, Dar es Salaam, which.
The plan was cancelled in 1972, in part due to its failure to adequately address the historical and social problems associated with the city.

In 1974, after a nationwide party referendum, the Tanzanian government announced that the capital would be moved from Dar es Salaam to a more central location to create significant social and economic improvements for.

The official capital since 1996, Dodoma was envisaged as the first non-monumental capital city as opposed to the monumentality and hierarchy of other planned capital cities such as Abuja, Yamoussoukro, Brasília and Washington, D.C.

history.label: Growth
history.text: The layout followed the typical colonial planning of the time with a European quarter segregated from a native village.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Its location in a rural environment was seen as the ujamaa heartland and therefore appropriate for a ujamaa capital that could see and learn from neighbouring villages and maintain a close relationship to the.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: A new capital was seen as a more economically viable alternative than attempting to reorganise and restructure Dar es Salaam and was idealised as a way of diverting development away from continued concentration in.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Objectives for the new capital included: that the city become a symbol of Tanzania's social and cultural values and aspirations; that the capital city function be supplemented by industrial-commercial development; and that the mistakes.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The final location of the parliament was not in its original intended location in the master plan, with that location now being developed as a site for a university.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: As a result, many government offices remain in Dar es Salaam, which remains the commercial and the de facto capital of Tanzania.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Dodoma was envisaged as a nation-building project to cement a newly post-colonial independence identity and direction in Tanzania, and is similar to projects in Nigeria (Abuja), Côte d'Ivoire (Yamoussoukro), Botswana (Gaborone), Malawi (Lilongwe) and.
